description: Evaluation set for the sample resume (Priya Narayan). Each item lists the resume section(s) that genuinely contain the answer (used for retrieval precision@k) plus reference facts (used for LLM-based faithfulness scoring). 'expected_refusal' marks questions the resume cannot answer.

questions.question: How many years of experience does the candidate have?
questions.reference: 8 years of experience.

questions.question: Which vector databases has the candidate worked with?
questions.reference: Pinecone, Chroma, FAISS, and Weaviate.

questions.question: What is the candidate's current job title and employer?
questions.reference: Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Flipkart.

questions.question: How much did the candidate reduce model serving latency at Flipkart?
questions.reference: From 240ms to 95ms, using ONNX runtime and request batching.

questions.question: What programming languages does the candidate know?
questions.reference: Python, Go, SQL, Java, and Bash.

questions.question: Tell me about the candidate's work at Swiggy.
questions.reference: Machine Learning Engineer at Swiggy (2018-2021): recommendation model raising conversion 11%, Airflow feature pipeline over 2TB/day, migrated batch inference to Kubernetes cutting cost 27%.

questions.question: What degrees has the candidate earned and from where?
questions.reference: M.Tech in Computer Science from IIT Bombay (2014-2016) and B.Tech in IT from NIT Trichy (2010-2014).

questions.question: What certifications does the candidate hold?
questions.reference: AWS Certified Machine Learning - Specialty (2022) and Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer (2021).

questions.question: Describe the Resume RAG System project.
questions.reference: Open-source resume QA system using Chroma, hybrid retrieval, and a cross-encoder reranker; 0.91 retrieval precision@5.

questions.question: How large a team has the candidate led?
questions.reference: Led a team of up to 6 engineers and mentored 3 junior ML engineers.

questions.question: What MLOps tools is the candidate familiar with?
questions.reference: Docker, Kubernetes, MLflow, Airflow, AWS SageMaker, and GCP Vertex AI.

questions.question: What did the candidate achieve in fraud detection?
questions.reference: RealTime Fraud Detection (2022): Kafka + Flink at 50k transactions/second with sub-100ms latency.

questions.question: By how much did the retrieval-augmented search assistant reduce support escalations?
questions.reference: Reduced customer support escalations by 32%.

questions.question: What languages does the candidate speak?
questions.reference: English (fluent), Hindi (native), Kannada (conversational).

questions.question: What improvement did the hybrid retrieval pipeline deliver over the FAISS baseline?
questions.reference: Improved search relevance (nDCG@10) by 18% over the previous FAISS baseline.

questions.question: Where is the candidate based?
questions.reference: Bengaluru, India.

questions.question: What deep learning frameworks does the candidate use?
questions.reference: PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, and Hugging Face Transformers.

questions.question: What was the candidate's role and main result at Mu Sigma?
questions.reference: Data Scientist (2016-2018): churn prediction models with 0.84 AUC and automated dashboards saving 15 hours/week.

questions.question: How many monthly active users did the candidate's systems serve?
questions.reference: 40M+ monthly active users.

questions.question: What streaming/data technologies does the candidate know?
questions.reference: Spark, Kafka, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, and Redis.

questions.question: What is the candidate's expected salary?
questions.reference: Not stated in the resume.

questions.question: Does the candidate speak French?
questions.reference: Not stated; the resume lists English, Hindi, and Kannada only.

questions.question: What is the candidate's current notice period?
questions.reference: Not stated in the resume.
